title: Flannel Static Wall
prompt: Create an original grunge track at 112 BPM in D minor, with sludgy verse tension, explosive chorus release, and rough human swing. Use heavy kick, cracked snare, loose hats, tom-heavy fills; bass grinds with distorted root motion and ugly slides. Guitars: dirty power chords, quiet clean verses, thick fuzz choruses, plus feedback squeals and abrupt dynamic drops. Vocal texture: raw, strained, raspy, vulnerable but not theatrical. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: gritty, live, dark, unpolished; mix/master: massive chorus guitars, loud snare, clear bass mud control, no glossy sheen.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop

title: Basement Fuse Burn
prompt: Create an original hardcore punk track at 190 BPM in D major, with fast aggressive drive, blunt riffing, and short explosive sections. Use rapid kick, hard snare, crashing hats, short fills; bass hammers root notes with minimal movement and maximum pressure. Guitars: downstroke power chords, short chromatic riffs, abrasive pick attack, plus feedback starts, stop-start hits, and no decorative solos. Vocal texture: shouted, raw, direct, urgent, close to distortion. Structure: drum pickup into the main riff, verse that strips guitars to one side, build with cymbals and bass movement, chorus that hits harder and opens the melody, breakdown with stops and feedback, then final chorus ending on a raw chord. Production: dry, loud, compressed, confrontational; mix/master: sharp snare, tight guitars, fast impact, no polished pop gloss.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop

title: Glass Throat Architecture
prompt: Create an original post-hardcore track at 152 BPM in C minor, with volatile dynamics, angular riffs, and emotional build-release structure. Use hard kick, sharp snare, shifting hats, tom bursts; bass moves between tense held notes and aggressive rhythmic figures. Guitars: jagged clean lines, dissonant chords, heavy chorus walls, plus feedback swells, stop-time breaks, and counter-rhythm guitar layers. Vocal texture: strained, expressive, alternating melodic and shouted textures. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: intense, layered, dynamic, modern; mix/master: clear attack, wide guitar depth, strong snare, controlled chaos.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop

title: Iron Glass Gravity
prompt: Create an original alternative metal track at 100 BPM in B minor, with heavy groove, melodic tension, and thick low-tuned guitar impact. Use huge kick, cracking snare, tight hats, tom-heavy transitions; bass locks with the guitar chugs while adding deep sub weight. Guitars: low-tuned palm-muted riffs, wide chorus chords, melodic lead hooks, plus feedback swells, clean bridge textures, and tight breakdown stops. Vocal texture: powerful, dark, controlled, melodic with grit. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: modern, heavy, precise, cinematic; mix/master: tight low guitars, punchy drums, clear vocal center, loud master without mud.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop

title: Concrete Bounce Riff
prompt: Create an original nu-metal groove rock track at 92 BPM in F minor, with syncopated groove, drop-tuned guitar weight, and rhythmic vocal pressure. Use punchy kick, tight snare, dry hats, tom punches; bass follows the groove with thick low-end stabs and short slides. Guitars: chunky muted riffs, scratchy rhythmic accents, wide chorus chords, plus turntable-like guitar noises made by pick scrapes, and clean bridge chords. Vocal texture: rhythmic, gritty, intense, partly spoken but not rap-dominant. Structure: noise-and-riff intro, lean verse with bass and dry drums, pre-chorus with harmony guitar and snare lift, full chorus with wider chords, eight-bar bridge or solo break, then final chorus into tight outro. Production: dry, heavy, percussive, modern; mix/master: tight chugs, strong snare, controlled sub, no muddy guitars.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, EDM drop, trap hi-hats, over-polished pop sheen, fake crowd chants, brittle cymbals, muddy guitars, weak snare, generic radio pop
